perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the ter m “Confidential Information” shall include, but not be limited to, documents, records, information and data (whether verbal, electronic or written), drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, schedules, product plans, marketing plans, technical proced ures, manufacturing processes, analyses, compilations, studies, software, prototypes, samples, formulas, methodologies, formulations, product developments, patent applications, know -how, experimental results, specifications and other business information, relating to the Party’s business, assets, operations or contracts, furnished to the other Party and/or the other Party’s affiliates, employees, officers, owners, agents, consultants or representatives, in the course of their work contemplated in this Agree ment, regardless of whether such Confidential Information has been expressly designated as confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the ter m “Confidential Information” shall include documents and records.
perturbation.explanation: By severely restricting the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include 'documents and records', the agreement creates an in-text contradiction. Section IX on Enforcement assumes a 'unique and sensitive nature' of Confidential Information that is significantly undermined by this limited definition. This creates uncertainty because the 'unique and sensitive nature' implies a wider scope that the agreement initially agrees with, which will be a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only 'documents and records'. The contradicted text acknowledges the 'unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information'. This limited definition directly contradicts the assumption of a broader, more encompassing nature of confidential information as implied in the contradicted section. The original text includes a range of items beyond just documents and records. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the enforcement clause is based on the nature of the original Confidential Information, but that original definition has been drastically reduced. The altered definition means the assumption in the contradicted clause is no longer fully valid, and it does cause confusion about how to interpret the agreement because the parties acknowledge a very specific 'unique and sensitive nature' that does not align with the new information. Therefore, it's a YES.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information with reasonable care. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the standard of care from 'strictest of confidence' to 'reasonable care', the agreement introduces ambiguity and contradicts the enforcement clause (Section IX). The 'unique and sensitive nature' of confidential information as asserted in Section IX implies a higher duty of care than merely 'reasonable care'. If something goes wrong, and damages occur, it creates uncertainty in the application of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the 'strictest of confidence,' while the changed text only requires 'reasonable care.' The contradicted text states that the confidential information is of a 'unique and sensitive nature' and that any breach would cause 'irreparable harm.' The change from 'strictest of confidence' to 'reasonable care' creates a contradiction because 'reasonable care' is a lower standard than the 'unique and sensitive nature' implies, and therefore creates ambiguity on which standard would be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement outlines the general understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'complete understanding' to 'general understanding' in the integration clause creates uncertainty. Section IX describes seeking damages. If the general agreement in this section does not completely agree with seeking damages for harms that may or may not be laid out in the text, there may be a problem.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement expresses the 'complete understanding' which is then changed to 'general understanding'. This undermines the clause related to seeking damages for breach because the 'general understanding' creates uncertainty about the scope of the agreement and what constitutes a breach that would cause irreparable harm. The contradicted text entitles the harmed party to 'all remedies available at law,' which is difficult to assess if the understanding of the agreement is now 'general' and not 'complete,' which is in line with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition as essential language was altered creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If a court finds that any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall be interpreted so as best to affect the intent of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: If a court finds that any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, the Parties will attempt to renegotiate the invalid portion.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the 'best to affect the intent of the Parties' clause with a 'renegotiation' clause introduces a contradiction. The original clause suggests a fallback interpretation, while the altered clause suggests a complete restart for that provision. This creates an issue when related to the Enforcement clause in Section IX because the parties can not renegotiate in cases of irreparable harm that has already happened. This creates a contradiction because renegotiation assumes time which may or may not exist when harm occurs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear fallback for interpreting the agreement if a provision was invalid. The changed text introduces a renegotiation clause, which directly contradicts the 'all remedies available at law' provision in the contradicted text. Specifically, the contradicted text allows for immediate equitable relief for irreparable harm, whereas the changed text introduces a time-consuming renegotiation process which may prevent an effective remedy due to time, thus contradicting the availability of all remedies at law for harm that has already occurred.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that any breach of this Agreement may cause harm for which damages may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to remedies available at law.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'irreparable harm' and 'equitable relief' creates a contradiction with the 'Governing Law' section. By removing irreparable harm from the situation and making the parties seek damages, the parties are agreeing to a situation that may not be possible to find monetary compensation for. This contradicts the governing law in Section X because Florida Law may allow for 'irreparable harm' or 'equitable relief'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed under the laws in the State of Florida .
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for 'equitable relief' and acknowledged 'irreparable harm.' The changed text removes these possibilities and seeks only 'damages available at law.' The contradicted text states that the agreement is governed by Florida law. Because Florida law may allow for equitable relief or recognize that irreparable harm occurred, the changed text's limitation to only monetary damages directly contradicts the governing law clause by removing remedies potentially available under that law. This presents a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed under the laws in the State of Florida .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be interpreted according to principles of fairness and good faith.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'governed under the laws in the State of Florida' with 'principles of fairness and good faith' removes the legal framework. This clashes with the 'Enforcement' clause (Section IX) because enforcement relies on legal standards, not subjective 'fairness'. The courts may not agree with the parties idea of fairness and this create a contradiction in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Florida law governs the agreement. The changed text replaces this with principles of fairness and good faith. The contradicted text states that 'The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law'. By removing the governing law (Florida law) and replacing it with 'principles of fairness and good faith', the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, because the harmed party may not be entitled to remedies available at law because there is no law governing the agreement. The enforcement clause relies on a legal framework, which is now absent, creating a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction.
